rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF FRANKLIN GOLDBERG AND JUNA GOLDBERG VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 3, 2013, Line 3, PLAINTIFFs ESTATE OF FRANKLIN GOLDBERG AND JUNA GOLDBERG, JUNA GOLDBERG, AS THE PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE OF ESTATE OF FRANKLIN GOLDBERG AND JUNA GOLDBERG, JUNA GOLDBERG'S Motion To Tax Costs. Granted in part and denied in part. The Court strikes $6,078.94 in expert fees and $3,944.78 for records obtained by subpoena. The record on this motion is insufficient to establish that the expert costs were incurred in "preparation for trial" within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure section 998(c)(1). The Court denies as to the travel costs. They are recoverable under Code of Civil Procedure section 1033.5(a)(3) and defendant sufficiently establishes the costs. rulings.Case Title: MARCIA GIUSTI et al VS. BKCM ENTERPRISES INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORAION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Def'S Notice Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deposition Of Pltff Marcia Giusti; Req For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 3, 2013, Line 2. DEFENDANTS JOHN SIMONSE, MAGNATE FUND #1 LLC, a California limited liability company, MAGNATE FUND #2 LLC, a California limited liability company, MAGNATE FUND #3 LLC, a California limited liability company, 27TH STREET ASSOCIATES LLC, 20 PARKRIDGE LLC, SOUTH VAN NESS ASSOCIATES LLC, 55 WOODWARD, LLC, a California limited liability company, Defendants' Motion to Compel Deposition of Plaintiff Marcia Giusti; Request for Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is denied. No sanctions to either party. Moving party took the deposition off calendar. Accordingly, there are no valid grounds for a motion to compel attendance under CCP 2025.410 et seq., which is the only basis for this motion (See Notice of Motion). The parties are ordered to appear at the hearing on May 3, 2013 prepared to meet and confer to select a timely date for Ms. Giusti's deposition. Defendants' Objections to Evidence are denied as moot. The Court did not rely on Plaintiff's declarations in arriving at this decision. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RASHID ALATRAQCHI VS. METRO CAB LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 3, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANT METRO CAB LLC, RICHARD HYBELS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the first and second causes of action for discrimination. Defendant correctly cites Gov't 12926(d) for the proposition that it must "regularly employ[] five or more persons" to be considered an "employer" subject to FEHA and a discrimination claim. Defendant's undisputed material fact 15 establishes that defendant only employed 2 persons. Plaintiff's response to that fact does not cite to any contrary admissible evidence. The Court denies summary adjudication as to the third cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress, sixth cause of action for harassment, and the prayer for punitive damages. Pursuant to Gov't Code §§ 12940(j)(1) and (j)(4)(A), Plaintiff can maintain the harassment cause of action even if he is an independent contractor. At pages 10:20-27, 31:2-34:21, 41:21-42:27 of Plaintiff's supplemental declaration, Plaintiff describes conduct that can constitute harassment. Defendant's evidentiary objection is only to the entirety of that document, and is overruled because at least some portions of it are admissible. The Court grants summary adjudication as to the breach of contract and wrongful termination causes of action. Even if the Court assumes that the written contract was forged, Plaintiff has not presented evidence sufficient to create an implied-in-fact contract that could be terminated only for cause. The mere statement at the outset that Plaintiff could keep working as long as he paid the gate fee cannot reasonably be read to limit termination to good cause and is insufficient to overcome the normal rule that working relationships are at will. Moreover, Plaintiff has testified that he had no contract with Defendant at all. In any event, even if the Court assumes an oral contract with a good cause discharge covenant governed the parties' relationship, the undisputed evidence establishes that defendant had good cause to end the relationship. Defendant's undisputed material fact 14 establishes that plaintiff's accidents and increased costs were the basis for the termination. In his opposition statement, Plaintiff does not point to any controverting admissible evidence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: ROY POOLE VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL FOUNDATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 3, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS (DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER)'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to Plaintiff's second cause of action for medical battery in his second amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has not pled facts showing Defendant performed a type of treatment substantially different from that for which it had any necessary consent. Indeed, Plaintiff alleges continuing express consent to restraint, which is inconsistent with his argued, but unpleaded, contention of withdrawal of consent. Plaintiff would not now be permitted to allege that he did not ask that his wrist be re-tied, and his allegation that he suffered injury from the manner in which he was restrained during treatment of his seizure would constitute negligence, not battery. Plaintiff has not requested leave to amend or argued any facts that could be pleaded in good faith that could allow this claim to go forward. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: AS YOU SOW, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT VS. UNIFIED MARINE INC. DBA SEASENSE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton In Support Of Motion Tocompel Further Responses To Pltf'S As You Sow'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories Adn Reqeust For Production And Inspection Of Documents, Set No One And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 3, 2013, Line 6. PLAINTIFF AS YOU SOW'S, a California non-profit public benefit corporation, Motion in Support of Motion to Compel Further Responses to Plaintiff's As You Sow's First Set of Special Interrogatories and Request for Production and Inspection of Documents, Set No. One and for Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted in part and denied in part. Special Interrogatory No. 34/Request for Production No. 16-Most, if not all, the relevant documents responsive to these requests were either produced before the Motion to Compel was filed or were promised before that date and have since been produced. With regard to the remaining documents: Defendant shall, within 10 court days, produce relevant documents related to the sale or marketing of liquid electrical tape by LH French and any other on-line retailer. Documents in possession of LH French are not in the custody and control of defendant and need not be produced. Request for Production No. 20-California Rules of Court 3.724(8) requires the parties to meet and confer with respect to Electronically Stored Information (ESI) before the initial case management conference. In the absence of a specific reference to this in the party's declarations, the Court assumes that a meaningful conference concerning ESI has not occurred. The parties must fully comply with CRC 3.724(8) before the Court will consider ordering a search of Defendant's server and computer system. No sanctions are awarded. Defendant had substantial justification for its opposition. Brimer v. Faygo 2007 WL 4285296, while a jurisdiction case and not citable, provides justification for Defendant's position that it had no expectation that its product would be sold to California consumers. See, Carretti v. Italpast (2002) 101 Cal App 4th 1236, 1246-1247. rulings.Case Title: HENRY WOLF VS. BMW OF NORTH AMERICA, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Henry Wolf'S Motion For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 3, 2013, Line 14, Plaintiff Henry Wolf'S Motion For A Protective Order. Granted, in part, and denied, in part. Within 20 days, Plaintiff shall produce all documents responsive to Requests for Production, nos. 2 - 8, 10, 11, 13 - 15, 17 - 19, 21 - 31, 33. Plaintiff is permitted to properly designate as "Confidential" any documents responsive to Requests for Production nos. 2 - 8, 15, 26, 29 - 31, 33 per the Stipulated Confidentiality Agreement and Protective Order issued on February 22, 2013. Plaintiff is also permitted to designate as "Confidential" pursuant to that Order any documents that have been or will be produced by third parties in response to Defendant's November 21, 2012 records subpoenas to Dr. Motzkin, Marin General Hospital and UCSF Medical Center. No sanctions are awarded. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: JOE DRUMMOND et al VS. FRANCESCA ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Jay Weiner'S Motion To Strike 2nd Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 3, 2013, Line 15, Defendant Jay Weiner's Motion To Strike 2nd Amended Complaint. Denied. Defendants have met their initial burden of proof to demonstrate that Plaintiffs' third cause of action for fraud arises from Defendants' exercise of free speech or petition rights. (CCP § 425.16(e).) The alleged misrepresentations were made at the HOA meeting on June 21, 2011, which is a public forum. (Damon v. Ocean Hills Journalism Club (2000) 85 Cal.App.4th 468, 475.) The HOA meeting is distinguishable from Turner v. Vista Pointe Ridge Homeowners Association (2009) 180 Cal.App.4th 676, 687 because it involves a public issue, a vote for a special assessment for roof work. Plaintiffs have met their shifting burden to show a likelihood of prevailing on the merits. Each party has presented contradictory evidence, and the court does not weigh Defendant's evidence against Plaintiff's in terms of either credibility or persuasiveness. Plaintiffs' evidence is sufficient, if ultimately believed, to meet the relatively low level of stating a prima facie case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: NICOLE DEMEO VS. MOHAMED ELHILAA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order Directing Japera Jenkins To Appear For A Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 3, 2013, Line 8. DEFENDANTS MOHAMED ELHILAA and MARTIN SMITH'S Motion for an Order Directing Japera Jenkins to Appear for a Deposition Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. Japera Jenkins shall appear at her deposition on May 10, 2013 at the office of counsel for defendants. No sanctions are awarded. The appropriate remedy for damages is pursuant to CCP 2020.240. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CAPITAL ONE BANK (USA) N.A. VS. MICHAEL LY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 3, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT MICHAEL LY's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Granted. Defendant has provided a sufficient declaration by his attorney stating that an answer was not timely filed due to the attorney's mistake. Relief is mandatory under these circumstances. In any event, relief was promptly sought and discretionary relief would be appropriate. Defendant's attorney is ordered to pay $202.50 for Plaintiff's fees and costs associated with obtaining the default. rulings.Case Title: TOM CORCORAN et al VS. SFS 39, INC.(DBA THE FRANCISCAN CRAB RESTAURANT), et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Sfs 39, Inc.(Dba The Franciscan Crab Restaurant), Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 3, 2013, Line 25, Defendant SFS 39, Inc. (Dba The Franciscan Crab Restaurant) Motion To Compel Arbitration. The Court grants the motion to compel arbitration and stays this action pending arbitration. The broad arbitration clauses apply to all of plaintiffs' claims. The Court recognizes that the issue concerning enforceability of an agreement to waive the right to bring a representative PAGA action is pending before the Supreme Court and that state and federal courts have disagreed on the issue. The arbitration agreement, however, also gives the arbitrator the authority to decide issues of enforcement, validity, scope, and applicability of the parties' agreement. Therefore, it will be for the arbitrator to decide whether to enforce the representative action waiver and whether it is appropriate for plaintiffs' representative PAGA claims to go forward in arbitration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: COREY J. GARDNER et al VS. HORNBLOWER YACHTS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arb Adn To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, Stay The Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 3, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANTs HORNBLOWER YACHTS, INC., TRACY SALVADOR, LEESA SINGER and LARISA THOMAS' Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, Stay The Proceedings. The motion to compel arbitration is granted. The Court stays this action. Plaintiffs agreed to arbitrate the instant dispute by executing the ACKNOWLEDGEMENT OF POLICIES and ACKNOWLEDGEMENT & AGREEMENT. Plaintiffs have not met their burden of demonstrating a waiver. Defendants moved to arbitrate almost immediately after they were served with the complaint. There is no authority providing that a defendant needs to seek arbitration prior to the commencement of litigation. Defendant's agents have the right to compel arbitration. See Dryer v. LA Rams, (1985) 40 Cal.3d 406, 418. Even assuming that plaintiffs demonstrate that the contracts are procedurally unconscionable, they fail to demonstrate substantive unconscionability because the agreements are mutual and preserve all rights and remedies the parties would have had in court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
